You go out on the football pitch, you want to play and to do well. I don’t understand. Do you really think that you sit in the dressing room before the game and you think ‘Oh, I have only one year to go - I will not play well today’? Where does that come from? When you’re a football player you want to go out and play and do as well as you can. What has that to do with the length of your contract? It’s a bit amazing. How long do you know that you will work for your company? As long as you work, you do well.

But the former Tottenham boss reckons Arsenal will be crowded out of the top-four again.

He told The Sun on Sunday: “I saw a lot of Alexandre Lacazette on TV and you would be surprised if he wasn’t right up there in the scoring charts.


“Despite bagging Lacazette I think the Gunners are short defensively, while Mesut Ozil has to do more on a regular basis.

Bollywood beauty Esha Gupta lets slip Arsenal are about to announce another signing

Gupta, who dated Arsenal right-back Hector Bellerin last year, was helping unveil Arsenal’s new blue away kit.

During an Instagram live chat she said: “I’m not sure if I should say the name but we are signing another player and I’m sure the boss is gonna do well this time.”

Gunners fans reacted with disbelief – and a good dose of humour.

Gupta, who was Miss India International 2007, then expanded on the incident during an interview with the Times of India.

“I’ve been an Arsenal fan for as long as I can remember.

“Like every Indian I’m crazy about cricket but football has also intrigued me and I like its history and geography.

“Modelling the team jersey was an opportunity too good to miss.”

Arsenal boss Arsene Wenger has been trying to sign Monaco winger Thomas Lemar for £45 million.

And Gupta appeared to confirm the deal was being thrashed out.

“Talks are on with Thomas Lemar and I hope he comes on board too. We need good players like him,” she said.

雖然西維爾在四角賽2戰2勝，但因這個賽制特別的比賽入波同樣計分，令冠軍最終落在士哥較多的阿仙奴之上，對此賓耶達便在Twitter留言表示疑問，卻換來一名「廠迷」責罵。
